calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-05-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Buggy, via Zoom, for Rita Labrie, Administrator.

The court and counsel discuss why the half siblings were not disclosed until final distribution and why the half siblings signed disclaimers which they now argue are invalid. Counsel offers testimony regarding the discrepancy reporting that he did not believe that the half siblings were required to be referenced in the initial petition but that he has spoken to all parties, including the half siblings, to confirm their individual interests in the estate and refers to information included in the Declarations RE Disclaimer of Interest and Memorandum of Points and Authorities filed with the court. The court confirms with counsel that his client currently resides at the real property, the only asset of the estate, and that currently there is no plan to sell it.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is taken under submission. The court notes that it will require a new order and instructs counsel to submit a revised order by noon, this Thursday, May-05-2022, to the assigned Examiner. Counsel confirms the court's instructions.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Andam. Not Reported.
